chunks.text: The breeze felt pleasant, and I observed the trees in the garden swaying slowly.
Grandmother made tea and had me hold the cup.
She always permits me to read any book I like.
Today she let me rest as long as I wanted.
I could notice petals drift on the wind.
I could hear a distant bird sing beyond the hedge.
" Close your eyes and allow the wind to carry you," grandmother said gently.
Grandfather used to admire the plum tree in our garden.
Grandmother often recalls that memory and smiles quietly.
I felt she wanted to encourage me to remember him.
Soon the evening sun painted the roof in gentle tones.
The garden became calm and golden.
Grandmother handed me a fresh cup.
I wished she would let me stay on the porch forever.
